strengths: Accurate definitions of temporal/spatial coherence align with standard physics
Clear distinction between coherence and intensity/frequency
Useful analogies with explicit phase relationship explanations
Correct mathematical formulations for phase difference and coherence length

issues.problem: Metronome analogy risks implying coherence requires perfect synchronization rather than constant phase relationship
issues.suggested_fix: Add clarification that 'perfect unison' is an extreme example, and emphasize that any constant phase relationship (even with offset) qualifies as coherence

issues.location: How this connects to the rest of physics
issues.problem: Quantum coherence connection is mentioned but not elaborated
issues.suggested_fix: Add a brief note explaining that while classical coherence and quantum coherence share mathematical similarities, they describe different physical phenomena

best_next_revision: Refine the metronome analogy to explicitly state that any constant phase relationship (not just perfect synchronization) qualifies as coherence, and expand the quantum coherence connection to clarify the distinction between classical and quantum coherence.
